lines: ## VERDICT -- does the within-year TI STRUCTURE reproduce 2020->2021? [VERIFIED within-year]
family OOS-xBH rank-transfer: Spearman(2020,2021) = 0.5 (does NOT reproduce -- family ordering is regime-transient too)
iron-buys-DD-not-net: iron REDUCES DD in ALL families 2020=True / 2021=True (SAME sign -> the iron mechanism reproduces)
OOS regime: BH OOS-net median = 49.0% (2020 Q4) / 6.3% (2021 Q4). configs beating BH on OOS net = 0 (2020) / 171 (2021).

EXPOSURE CONTROL (the referee catch): 2021-Q4 BH was only ~6.3% (post-ATH DECLINE/chop, not a bull); the beat-BH configs there have median OOS time-in = 0.32 -- LOW time-in => they beat BH by SITTING IN CASH during the decline (EXPOSURE-timing/preservation, NOT alpha -- the going-to-cash mechanism), not genuine signal. So '2021 OOS beats BH' is the SAME de-risked-beta mechanism paying in a down-quarter, NOT a new edge (it LOSES to BH in the 2020 bull-OOS). 2020 vs 2021 = the two SIDES of de-risked beta.
robust band participates (robust_frac>0) in every family both years: True

HEADLINE: the within-year TI structure PARTIALLY reproduces 2020->2021 -- see the per-stat breakdown. This is the WITHIN-YEAR structure (distinct from the cross-year config TRANSLATION, which is ~0). The deployable read is unchanged: the iron buys DRAWDOWN + robustness, NOT bull-net (0 beat BH); the family band is a de-risked beta that participates both years.

MULTIPLICITY NOTE: thousands of (TI x config x TF) cells were scanned; the per-family numbers are the ROBUST-BAND (TRAIN&VAL-positive) OOS aggregate, NOT a cherry-picked best. A single 'best config' claim would need Deflated-Sharpe vs the N tried -- not made here (the band, not the #1, is the unit).
